input.content:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
The last piece is the browser client in /app/www/app.js. Point fetch at the same-origin API base /api/notes. When the script runs, load the list into the notes-list element. Each row needs edit and delete buttons whose class lists include edit-btn and delete-btn.

Submitting note-form should create a note when nothing is selected and update the active row otherwise. When create or save succeeds, note-title and note-content should be empty and the form should be ready for a new note on the next submit rather than staying on the previous row. Delete must call the API and refresh the list. After every successful POST, PUT, or DELETE, reload the list so the page matches the database.

Send Content-Type application/json and the trimmed API key from /app/config/api.key on POST, PUT, and DELETE only, not on GET. The automated frontend harness is not a real browser; read /app/docs/js_verifier_mock.md for DOM and fetch quirks before you declare done. Keep /app.js reachable over HTTP from the running server on port 8080, not only on disk.


Current terminal state:
New Terminal Output:

root@08d5c4164ec3:/app#

Current Terminal Screen: document.getElementById('note-form').addEventListener('submit', async function(e) { e.preventDefault(); var title = document.getElementById('note-title').value; var content = document.getElementById('note-content').value; await fetch(API, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ title: title, content: content }) }); fetchNotes(); }); fetchNotes(); root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# sed -n '1,200p' /app/www/index.html Notes App

Notes

root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# cat /app/config/api.key tb3_notes_api_secret_2026 root@08d5c4164ec3:/app#

New Terminal Output:

[]root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# sed -n '1,220p' /app/docs/api_contract.md
This document is the normative REST contract for the notes API. Automated checks use arbitrary title and content strings in requests; only the behaviors, status
 codes, headers, and JSON field names here matter.

Store notes in /app/data/notes.db. Read the shared secret from /app/config/api.key and trim surrounding whitespace before comparing it to the X-API-Key header o
n mutating requests. The stock api.key file ships with tb3_notes_api_secret_2026. POST, PUT, and DELETE require a matching key or the server responds unauthoriz
ed. GET never requires the key; a bad or missing key on a mutating call is unauthorized.

Every JSON response must use a JSON content type and include an access-control header that allows any origin. OPTIONS preflight must advertise GET, POST, PUT, D
ELETE, and OPTIONS, and must allow Content-Type and X-API-Key as request headers. Preflight may return empty or non-empty success.

POST /api/notes accepts JSON with string title and content. Title must be present and non-empty; an empty object or missing or blank title is a client error. Su
ccess is HTTP 201 with a body containing numeric id plus stored title and content. GET /api/notes returns a JSON array of objects with id, title, and content. O
ptional limit and offset query parameters are integers: default limit 50, maximum 100, default offset 0. /api/notes?limit=100 returns at most one hundred rows;
/api/notes?limit=999 is capped the same way. The total row count before paging goes in the X-Total-Count header.

GET /api/notes/{id} returns that object. A missing id returns HTTP 404 with JSON {"error":"not found"} using that exact lowercase message string, including for
ids such as /api/notes/99999 on GET, PUT, or DELETE. PUT uses the same field rules as POST, returns HTTP 200 with saved fields when the row exists, or the same
not-found JSON when it does not. Successful DELETE returns HTTP 200 with JSON {"status":"deleted"}; deleting a missing id returns the same not-found JSON as GET
. Reject POST or PUT bodies larger than 65536 bytes with HTTP 413 and a JSON error body.

SQLite access must remain correct under concurrent requests. Verifiers may reset /app/data/notes.db and send bursts of about fifteen parallel POST requests; eve
ry stored row must still appear in responses afterward.
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# curl -sS -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/notes -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-API-Key: tb3_notes_api_secret_2026' -d '{"tit
le":"Smoke","content":"Test"}'
{"error":"server error"}root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# curl -sS http://localhost:8080/api/notes
[]root@08d5c4164ec3:/app#

New Terminal Output:

[]root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# curl -i -sS -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/notes -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-API-Key: tb3_notes_api_secret_2026' -d '
{"title":"Smoke","content":"Test"}'
HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
Date: Tue, 26 May 2026 12:51:16 GMT
Content-Type: application/json
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS
Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, X-API-Key
Content-Length: 24

{"error":"server error"}root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# ss -ltnp | grep ':8080' || true
bash: ss: command not found
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# ps auxww | grep -E '(notes|8080|server|mhd|microhttpd)' | grep -v grep
bash: ps: command not found
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# ls -la /app/data || true
total 52
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root    62 May 26 12:50 .
drwxr-xr-x 10 root root   157 May 26 12:49 ..
-rw-r--r--  1 root root  4096 May 26 12:50 notes.db
-rw-r--r--  1 root root 32768 May 26 12:50 notes.db-shm
-rw-r--r--  1 root root 20632 May 26 12:50 notes.db-wal
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# ls -la /app/data/notes.db || true
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 4096 May 26 12:50 /app/data/notes.db
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# sqlite3 /app/data/notes.db '.tables' 2>/dev/null || echo 'sqlite3 not available or db missing/corrupt'
sqlite3 not available or db missing/corrupt
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app#

Current Terminal Screen:
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -O2 -Iinclude
LDFLAGS = -lmicrohttpd -lsqlite3

SOURCES = src/main.c src/routes.c src/static.c src/db.c src/auth.c
TARGET = notes_server

$(TARGET): $(SOURCES)
        $(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) $(SOURCES) $(LDFLAGS)

clean:
        rm -f $(TARGET)
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# ls -la /app
total 8
drwxr-xr-x 10 root root 137 May 26 12:52 .
drwxr-xr-x 20 root root 278 May 26 12:50 ..
-rw-r--r--  1 root root 269 May 26 12:42 Makefile
-rw-r--r--  1 root root 143 May 26 12:40 README.md
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  41 May 26 12:40 config
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  80 May 26 12:52 data
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  26 May 26 12:40 deps
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root 103 May 26 12:40 docs
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  37 May 26 12:40 include
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  22 May 26 12:40 sql
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root 136 May 26 12:49 src
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  55 May 26 12:50 www
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# make -C /app
make: Entering directory '/app'
gcc -Wall -Wextra -O2 -Iinclude -o notes_server src/main.c src/routes.c src/static.c src/db.c src/auth.c -lmicrohttpd -lsqlite3
make: Leaving directory '/app'
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# ls -la /app/notes_server
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 36248 May 26 12:53 /app/notes_server
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# nohup /app/notes_server >/app/data/server.log 2>&1 &
[1] 4014
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app# curl -sS -D - http://localhost:8080/app.js | sed -n '1,5p'
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Tue, 26 May 2026 12:53:13 GMT
Connection: close
Content-Type: application/javascript
Content-Length: 3370
root@08d5c4164ec3:/app#

. Reject POST or PUT bodies larger than 65536 bytes with HTTP 413 and a JSON error body.

SQLite access must remain correct under concurrent requests. Verifiers may reset /app/data/notes.db and send bursts of about fifteen parallel POST requests; eve
ry stored row must still appear in responses afterward.

le":"Smoke","content":"Test"}'

CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -O2 -Iinclude
LDFLAGS = -lmicrohttpd -lsqlite3

SOURCES = src/main.c src/routes.c src/static.c src/db.c src/auth.c
TARGET = notes_server

$(TARGET): $(SOURCES)
        $(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) $(SOURCES) $(LDFLAGS)

clean:
        rm -f $(TARGET)
